ogTitle: Bugle Hut vs Bugle Ranges: Climate Safety

heading: Bugle Hut vs Bugle Ranges — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Bugle Hut, South Australia and Bugle Ranges, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Bugle Hut has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Bugle Ranges (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Bugle Hut or Bugle Ranges safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Bugle Hut has a flood risk score of 43/100, while Bugle Ranges scores 23/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Bugle Hut scores 18/100 for bushfire risk. Bugle Ranges scores 53/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Bugle Hut or Bugle Ranges?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Bugle Hut has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.05% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
